mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns are positive in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.0% signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering rapid risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase quarter-over-quarter
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in >50% of cases over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 20-day returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, liquidity constraints tighten sharply, forcing rapid deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns remain positive in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS stays above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with a stable unemployment rate confirms sustainable employment expansion, supporting consumer spending and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising PAYEMS and stable UNRATE yields negative 60-day forward returns in >50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly translates to increased corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 against 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals imminent recessionary supply shocks, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines exceeding 1.0% during tightening cycles are followed by positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases.
